senses.definition: a variation of basketball adapted for players with disabilities who use wheelchairs, played with similar rules and objectives

examples.x: They formed a wheelchair basketball team at the community center.
He dribbled the ball skillfully in wheelchair basketball practice.
She made a great pass during the wheelchair basketball game.
The wheelchair basketball tournament attracted teams from all over.
She joined the wheelchair basketball league to stay active.

senses.definition: (basketball) a shot taken close to the basket where the player lays the ball into the hoop

examples.x: She made an easy layup after a quick break.
The point guard drove to the basket for a layup.
His layup tied the game in the final seconds.
They practiced layups during warm-ups.
A well-timed pass led to an open layup opportunity.

senses.definition: a high-energy shot in basketball where a player jumps and forcefully puts the ball through the basket

examples.x: The crowd cheered loudly after his impressive dunk.
His dunk gave the team a momentum boost.
The highlight reel showed his spectacular dunk.
A fast break led to an uncontested dunk.
The center's dunk electrified the home crowd.

senses.definition: (basketball) a shot taken while jumping in the air, typically outside the paint area, to score points

examples.x: The guard's jump shot tied the score.
His quick release on the jump shot surprised the defense.
She practiced her jump shot from mid-range.
They worked on jump shots during shooting drills.
A contested jump shot fell through the net.

senses.definition: (basketball) a shot made from beyond the three-point line, worth three points

examples.x: He made a clutch three-pointer to tie the game.
She practiced her three-pointers for hours each day.
The team celebrated after he sank a three-pointer.
He's known for his deadly accuracy with three-pointers.
She attempted a three-pointer to extend their lead.

senses.definition: an offensive play in basketball where one player sets a screen for a teammate with the ball, then moves toward the basket for a potential pass or shot

examples.x: The pick and roll created an open shot for the point guard.
They executed the pick and roll to perfection.
He scored easily off the pick and roll.
She passed to her teammate after the pick and roll.
They practiced the pick and roll extensively during training.

senses.definition: the act of a player retrieving the ball after a missed shot or free throw in basketball

examples.x: She led the team by securing ten rebounds.
He grabbed a crucial offensive rebound and scored.
Offensive rebounds resulted in second-chance points.
The forward aggressively pursued a crucial rebound.

senses.definition: (in basketball) to position oneself between an opponent and the basket to secure a rebound

examples.x: He used his strength to box out the taller player.
She learned how to box out effectively during practice.
He couldn't box out his opponent and lost the rebound.
The player failed to box out, allowing an easy putback.
She successfully boxed out her rival and grabbed the rebound.

senses.definition: a defensive strategy in basketball where each player guards a specific offensive player throughout the game

examples.x: Our team switched to man-to-man defense in the second half.
Man-to-man defense requires strong communication among players.
He's known for his tenacity in playing man-to-man defense.
We struggled to break their tight man-to-man defense.

senses.definition: a defensive strategy where two players from the defending team focus their efforts on guarding a single offensive player

examples.x: The star player was constantly facing a double team during the game.
The center managed to score despite facing a double team in the post.
The coach instructed the defenders to initiate a double team on the opposing team's center.
The point guard quickly passed the ball to his teammate to avoid the double team.
When LeBron drives to the basket, he often draws a double team.

senses.definition: (basketball) a shot taken from the free-throw line as a penalty for a foul, with no defenders allowed to interfere

examples.x: She made both free throws to tie the game.
He practiced free throws after practice every day.
They missed crucial free throws down the stretch.
She calmly sank the free throw under pressure.
The referee signaled for two free throws.

senses.definition: a timer used in sports, particularly basketball, that gives teams a limited time to shoot

examples.x: The shot clock was down to three seconds, so he had to take the shot quickly.
They couldn't find an open player before the shot clock expired, resulting in a turnover.
They struggled to get a good look at the basket as the shot clock ticked away.
With only five seconds left on the shot clock, she made a desperation three-pointer.
Time was running out on the shot clock, so they attempted a long-range shot.

senses.definition: (basketball) the act of moving without dribbling the ball, resulting in a turnover
a rule violation in basketball where a player dribbles the ball with both hands simultaneously or stops dribbling and then resumes dribbling again

examples.x: He was penalized for a double dribble.
The referee called a double dribble on the point guard.
She lost possession due to a double dribble.
They practiced avoiding double dribbles in drills.
His double dribble led to a change of possession.

senses.definition: (basketball) a penalty for unsportsmanlike behavior or rule

examples.x: He received a technical foul for arguing with the referee.
The coach was assessed a technical foul for excessive arguing.
Their team was penalized with a technical foul for delay of game.
The referee called a technical foul on the bench for excessive celebrations.
She was given a technical foul for using inappropriate language toward an opponent.

senses.definition: a successful scoring shot made during regular play, excluding free throws

examples.x: The player's accuracy in field goals contributed significantly to their win.
He made a spectacular field goal from beyond the three-point line.
The team's strategy revolves around high-percentage field goals close to the basket.
She missed the field goal attempt, but her teammate got the rebound.
He drove to the hoop and made a contested field goal with a layup.

senses.definition: a pass where the ball is thrown to the floor so that it bounces to a teammate in basketball

examples.x: He used a bounce pass to get the ball past the defender.
The point guard made a quick bounce pass to the center.
A strong bounce pass can maintain the team's offensive momentum.
She executed a perfect bounce pass to her teammate under the basket.
During practice, they focused on improving their bounce pass accuracy.

senses.definition: (basketball) a throw made by pushing the ball from the chest using both hands directly to a teammate

examples.x: He made a quick chest pass to his teammate to start the fast break.
The chest pass is effective for short, accurate throws.
Practice your chest pass to improve your passing accuracy.
A good chest pass requires strong arm muscles.
They scored after a perfect chest pass from the point guard.

senses.definition: a deceptive move in basketball where a player pretends to pass the ball to mislead the defender

examples.x: The point guard used a fake pass to confuse the defender.
He executed a perfect fake pass before driving to the basket.
A well-timed fake pass can create an open shot opportunity.
Coaches encourage players to use the fake pass to keep defenses guessing.
The defender fell for the fake pass, leaving the lane wide open.

senses.definition: a defensive strategy in basketball where three players form a triangle shape to guard against the offense's scoring opportunities

examples.x: The team switched to a triangle defense to better cover the opposing shooters.
In a triangle defense, communication between the three players is crucial.
Our team practiced the triangle defense extensively before the big game.
He positioned the players in a triangle defense to block passing lanes.
The triangle defense helped them force more turnovers during the game.

senses.definition: a defensive strategy in basketball that combines elements of different defensive tactics, such as zone and man-to-man defense

examples.x: Players must understand their roles within the combination defense to execute it successfully.
Coach implemented a combination defense to adapt to the opponent's versatile offense.
Players need to communicate well to execute a combination defense smoothly.
Learning to switch seamlessly between zone and man-to-man defense is key to mastering combination defense.
The team's combination defense stifled the opposing team's star player.

senses.definition: an offensive foul in basketball where an offensive player runs into a stationary defensive player

examples.x: The referee called a charging when the player bulldozed into the defender.

senses.definition: a move where a player stands still to block a defender and help a teammate get open
a shot where the ball bounces off the backboard before going into the basket
a one-handed basketball shot released with a pushing motion rather than a typical two-handed shot

examples.x: He scored with a quick push shot from the free-throw line.
She nailed the push shot over the defender's outstretched hand.
The point guard's specialty is his reliable push shot from mid-range.
The player used a push shot to avoid getting blocked by the taller defender.
Her push shot floated softly into the basket.

senses.definition: a one-handed shot made by swinging the ball in an arc over the player's head toward the basket

examples.x: He made an incredible hook shot over his defender’s head.
She practiced her hook shot until it became one of her best moves.
When he’s close to the basket, he often relies on his hook shot.
She missed the hook shot, but her teammate grabbed the rebound.
The player faked left and then quickly turned for a hook shot.

senses.definition: a defensive move where two players exchange their assignments to better guard their opponents
